skill.name: faceswap
skill.description: AI Face Swap - Swap face in video using verging.ai API. Supports local video files, YouTube/Bilibili URLs, local and remote face images. Auto-download, trimming, real-time progress.
skill.summary: Swap faces in videos using the verging.ai API with support for local files, YouTube/Bilibili URLs, and remote images.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation/instruction file (no executable code) that guides an AI assistant to run shell commands (yt-dlp, ffmpeg, curl) and make API calls to verging.ai. All 82 static findings are false positives in context: backtick patterns are markdown code examples, URLs are legitimate API endpoints, API key references are standard authentication documentation, and temp directory access is standard media processing. The combination of network + credentials + external commands is expected for this use case. No malicious intent detected. Risk level is medium due to the breadth of system access required.

medium_findings.title: Broad system access required
medium_findings.description: The skill requires yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl binaries plus the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable. While all usages are documented and legitimate for face-swap workflows, the combination of network access, external command execution, credential handling, and filesystem write access represents a significant attack surface if the skill were modified maliciously. This is a known characteristic of media-processing skills, not a current vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Temp directory usage in /tmp
low_findings.description: The skill uses /tmp/verging-faceswap/ for intermediate video and image files. This is standard practice for media processing but files may persist if not cleaned up. The documentation recommends cleanup. Low risk.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives: backtick patterns
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 44 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in markdown files. These are all code-block examples in documentation showing curl, ffmpeg, and yt-dlp commands. They are not executable code in the skill itself. False positive.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives: hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: 17 URL references flagged as suspicious are all legitimate API endpoints (verging.ai/api/v1/*) and documentation links. False positive.

low_findings.title: Static analyzer false positives: weak cryptography
low_findings.description: The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:33 are likely false positives triggered by the term 'key' in API key context or video codec references (libx264). No actual cryptographic operations are performed by the skill.

content.user_title: Swap Faces in Videos with AI
content.value_statement: Swapping faces in videos manually is slow and requires specialized software. This skill automates the entire workflow using the verging.ai API, accepting local files or YouTube/Bilibili URLs as input and delivering the result directly.
actual_capabilities: Process local video files or YouTube/Bilibili URLs as input
Accept local face images or remote image URLs for the swap target
Trim videos to a specified time range or auto-trim to 30 seconds maximum
Upload videos to verging.ai cloud storage via presigned R2 URLs
Poll job status at 5-second intervals and report progress in real time
Optionally auto-download the completed result to the current directory
limitations: Maximum video duration is 30 seconds (longer videos are auto-trimmed)
Requires a valid verging.ai API key with sufficient credits
Depends on external binaries: yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl must be installed
HD mode consumes 3 credits per second versus 1 credit per second for normal mode

use_cases.title: Content creator quick edits
use_cases.description: Swap a face in a short clip for social media content without opening a video editor.

use_cases.title: YouTube video face replacement
use_cases.description: Provide a YouTube URL and a face image, and get the swapped result automatically downloaded.

use_cases.title: Batch face-swap automation
use_cases.description: Use the CLI command repeatedly with different inputs to process multiple videos in sequence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic local file swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /faceswap with video ./video.mp4 and face image ./face.jpg to swap the face.

prompt_templates.title: YouTube URL with trimming
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /faceswap with video https://youtube.com/watch?v=xxx, face https://example.com/face.jpg, trimming from 10 to 25 seconds in HD mode and auto-downloading the result.

prompt_templates.title: Remote face image with custom output
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /faceswap with video ./input.mp4, a remote face image URL, and save the output to /tmp/result.mp4.

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline with credit check
prompt_templates.prompt: First check my verging.ai credit balance, then run /faceswap with the provided video and face URL, trim to 15 seconds, use HD mode, and download the result.

output_examples.input: Swap the face in a YouTube clip from 10-25 seconds in HD mode
output: Uploading trimmed video (15 seconds, HD = 45 credits)...

best_practices: Set the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill to avoid passing the key in commands
Trim videos to the shortest needed segment to minimize credit consumption
Use normal mode for previews and HD mode only for final output to save credits
Clean up /tmp/verging-faceswap/ after processing to avoid leaving media files on disk
anti_patterns: Do not pass the API key directly in the command line where it may appear in shell history or process listings
Do not use JSON body (-d) for the upload-video endpoint; it requires multipart form data (-F)
Do not attempt to upload the face image separately via /upload-video; the create-job endpoint accepts it directly as swap_image=@path

faq.question: What is the maximum video length supported?
faq.answer: 30 seconds. Videos longer than 30 seconds are automatically trimmed.

faq.question: How do I get a verging.ai API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and go to API Keys to create one.

faq.question: What external tools are required?
faq.answer: yt-dlp for downloading videos, ffmpeg and ffprobe for trimming, and curl for API requests.

faq.question: How much does a face swap cost?
faq.answer: Normal mode costs 1 credit per second. HD mode costs 3 credits per second.

faq.question: Can I use YouTube and Bilibili URLs?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports YouTube and Bilibili video URLs through yt-dlp.

faq.question: Where are temporary files stored?
faq.answer: Intermediate files are stored in /tmp/verging-faceswap/ and should be cleaned up after use.
